title: ClosedOperator
summary: Builds the closed OR linear combination from its coefficient system and packages its stated properties.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Builds the closed OR linear combination from its coefficient system and packages its stated properties.

mathematicalContent.heading: Formal boundary
mathematicalContent.body: This page describes exactly the declarations present in PolyGJMSoperators/OR/ClosedOperator.lean; claims not represented by a declaration are not counted as verified.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Definition: regular. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: def regular (N : ℕ) (n w₁ w₂ : ℚ) : OROperatorData

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: regular coeff System coeff. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem regular_coeffSystem_coeff (N : ℕ) (n w₁ w₂ : ℚ) (I : ORIndex N) : ((regular N n w₁ w₂).coeffSystem).coeff I = ORRegularCoeff n w₁ w₂ I

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Definition: regular Operator. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: def regularOperator (N : ℕ) (n w₁ w₂ : ℚ) (CalConf : Calculus Conf) : CalConf.BiOperator

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: regular is Bi Tangential of rational defect cancellation. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem regular_isBiTangential_of_rational_defect_cancellation {κ : Type _} [DecidableEq κ] (N : ℕ) (n w₁ w₂ : ℚ) {K : Finset κ} (E : (regular N n w₁ w₂).ORDefectExpansion CalConf K)

Primary curved ambient source for the encoded OR recurrence.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
